title: Request Smuggling, Response Splitting, Parser Differentials, and Downgrades · Execution Trace: Downgrade Consistency Gate
summary: A front end and origin disagree on length, whitespace, duplicate fields, target decoding, and line endings; the team tests each parser alone and forwards ambiguous messages after normalization. Planned artifact: Add and verify the downgrade consistency gate increment in multi-recipient parser-consistency threat model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Test HTTP/2 or HTTP/3 to HTTP/1.1 translation, proxy chains, cache parsing, and backend normalization with the same adversarial corpus.
Prevent attacker-controlled CRLF and control bytes from creating new fields, responses, logs, caches, or downstream protocol records.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Downgrade Consistency Gate · Theory Downgrade Consistency Gate
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory protocol team handles case 4948 in a municipal gateway receiving one-byte TCP fragments. Build deterministic Go evidence to reject framing and control-byte differentials before a message crosses recipients; preserve this operating constraint: the maintained Go protocol stack remains the production transfer target; then replace one field with repeated non-combinable lines and preserve their order. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
